RE
QUEST FOR QUOTATION
Date 05 February 2026
Department CTS
Requestor Moegamat Alexander
Due Date 16 February 2026
Contact Person Dillin Nel
Budget Available (not shown) 490 000
Cost Centre 8550
Account 43422
Quantity Specification
Developmen
t hours X
800
BACKGROUND
Cape Peninsula University of Technology (CPUT) is implementing Phase 3 of the Staff Development
Academy (SDA) . This phase includes the end- to- end development of a Customised AI Tutor Bot to
be integrated into the SDA’s Joomla 5–based online learning platform.
The AI Tutor Bot will deliver personalised, competency- based learning to CPUT staff, support real-
time assessment, generate analytics for WSP/ATR and BBBEE reporting, and comply with POPIA
and public- sector data governance standards.
CPUT now invites qualified vendors to submit quotations for the full development, integration, and
licensing of this AI Tutor Bot
SCOPE OF WORK
The selected vendor shall:
• Design, build, and deploy a custom AI Tutor Bot trained on CPUT- specific HR and policy
co
ntent (e.g., Code of Conduct, Disciplinary Procedures)
• I
ntegrate the bot with the existing SDA platform (Joomla 5) and CPUT HR systems (e.g.,
ITS/HRIS)
• Deliver a annual, on- premises licence with full source code and documentation
• Pro
vide user and admin training
• Offer 6 months of post- deployment technical support
3. TECHNICAL & FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS
T
he AI Tutor Bot must support the following features:
Natural Language Interaction Understand plain- English queries; maintain context across session
dialogue
Personalisation Adapt learning paths based on user role, progress, and performan
Content Integration Trainable on CPUT documents (PDF, Word, SCORM); auto- genera
from uploaded material
Assessment & Feedback Dynamic competency- based quizzes; real- time feedback; user rat
Admin & Reporting Dashboard for L&D and line managers; exportable reports for WS
DHET

pages.title: CPUT_Document.pdf
pages.text:
Compliance POPIA- compliant; no external data sharing; audit logs; role- based access
4. MANDATORY SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS
4.1 Skills Matrix
Bidders must provide a detailed skills matrix for their proposed project team, covering at
least the following roles:
Project Manager PMP/PRINCE2 certified; min. 3 years managing AI/Ed Tech projects i
sector or higher education
AI/NLP Developer(s) Proficiency in Python, Tensor Flow/Py Torch, Hugging Face, Rasa or L
experience fine- tuning LLMs on domain- specific content
Full- Stack
Developer(s) Joomla 5 plugin/module development; RESTful APIs; MySQL; Java Sc
Data Scientist(s) Experience building adaptive learning algorithms; analytics dashboa
Instructional
Designer(s)
Background in adult learning theory; experience converting HR poli
interactive e- learning
QA/Test Engineer(s) Automated/manual testing; UAT coordination; WCAG accessibility c
Attach CVs/resumes for all key personnel.
4.2 Company & Project History
Bidders must submit:
• A company profile (max. 2 pages)
• Summaries of at least 3 similar AI/Ed Tech projects delivered in the past 5 years,
including:
o Client name and sector (public/private/education)
o Project duration and team size
o Technologies used (e.g., LLMs, frameworks, LMS integrations)
o Measurable outcomes (e.g., user satisfaction, completion rates)
Preference will be given to vendors with public- sector or higher education experience.
4.3 Client References
Provide three (3) verifiable references for projects involving:
• AI chatbots or virtual tutors
• LMS or e- learning platform development
• Public- sector or university clients (preferred)
Include: Name, Organisation, Email, Phone. References may be contacted.
5. DELIVERABLES
The vendor must deliver:
1. Fully functional AI Tutor Bot integrated into SDA (Joomla 5)
2. Perpetual, modifiable, on- premises software licence
3. Full source code, model weights, and training pipelines

pages.title: CPUT_Document.pdf
pages.text:
4. Admin & user documentation
Prop
osals should be submitted to Dillin Nel via email to < Neld@cput.ac.za> by no later than the
16th February 2026.
Thank you for your interest in providing a solution to Cape Peninsula University of Technology.

plete the Proposal Document

ease complete the attached proposal as part of your proposal.

Com
pliance Documents
Pl
ease submit your quote together with the following company documents:
1. Tax PIN
2. V
alid BBB EE certificate / Affidavit
3. Valid COIDA / Letter of Good Standing
4. Completed SCM SBD Forms (SBD 4 and SBD 6.1)
5. Completed Declaration.
The above- mentioned documents except the BBB EE certificate are mandatory, failure to submit
may render your quotation non- responsive.
You will not be granted points for BBBEE certificate if you fail to submit a BBBEEE certificate.
Due date to submit quotation
Due Date: 16th February 2026
NB: LATE QUOTATIONS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED.
